prompts.label: Hello World and php -S
prompts.text:
I'm new to PHP 8.2+. Create the smallest useful starter.

- index.php printing Hello, World
- Run with php -S localhost:8000 in the project folder
- Explain <?php tags and that files outside public docroot should not be web-accessible

No frameworks yet.

prompts.label: Composer init and PSR-4 autoload
prompts.text:
Set up a tiny PHP project with Composer for a beginner.

- composer init, composer.json with PSR-4 autoload for src/
- One class App\Greeter in src/Greeter.php and index.php requiring vendor/autoload.php
- Commands: composer install, php index.php
- Explain vendor/ and why it belongs in .gitignore

Keep it minimal.

prompts.label: Types strict_types and scalars
prompts.text:
Teach PHP scalar types and strict mode.

- declare(strict_types=1); at top of a file
- Functions with int, string, bool parameters and return types
- Show one type error example and how PHP coerces without strict mode
- Mention union types string|int in one line

Short runnable scripts only.

prompts.label: Arrays foreach and array functions
prompts.text:
Explain PHP arrays (ordered map mental model) for beginners.

- Indexed and associative arrays, foreach
- array_map, array_filter, array_column on a list of user rows (name, email)
- Spread operator ... in function calls briefly
- One common mistake: += vs array_merge for lists

No framework.

prompts.label: Functions defaults and named args
prompts.text:
Create a small formatting helper module.

- greet(string $name, string $prefix = 'Hello'): string
- Nullable ?string and null coalescing ??
- Named arguments PHP 8 example
- When to use variadic ...$tags

Include 3 call examples with expected output.

prompts.label: OOP class constructor visibility
prompts.text:
Introduce OOP in plain PHP.

- class User with private properties, constructor promotion, public getter
- Method toArray() for debugging
- One paragraph: class vs object
- readonly properties PHP 8.2 mention on one field

Single file or src/ layout; no framework.

prompts.label: PDO SQLite CRUD
prompts.text:
Build a notes table with PDO and SQLite (no ORM).

- schema: id, title, body, created_at
- Functions: createNote, listNotes, deleteNote using prepared statements
- PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE => EXCEPTION
- Never concatenate user input into SQL — explain why

Provide schema.sql and notes.php CLI or simple HTML form optional.

prompts.label: Sessions and flash messages
prompts.text:
Teach PHP sessions for a tiny login demo (educational, not production auth).

- session_start(), $_SESSION['user'] after fake login form POST
- Logout unset and session_destroy
- One-time flash message pattern for success/errors
- session.cookie_httponly and secure flags — mention in checklist

Two pages: login.php and dashboard.php.

prompts.label: File upload validation
prompts.text:
Handle image upload safely in plain PHP.

- Form enctype multipart/form-data
- Check $_FILES error, size limit, finfo MIME type (not trusting extension only)
- move_uploaded_file to uploads/ outside web root or with .htaccess deny
- Randomize filename to avoid overwrites

List security mistakes beginners make.

prompts.label: Exceptions and custom types
prompts.text:
Replace die() with exceptions in a small script.

- Custom ValidationException with message
- try/catch/finally reading a config file
- Throwable vs Exception one sentence
- When to let exceptions bubble to a front controller

Runnable example under 50 lines.

prompts.label: Namespaces and use statements
prompts.text:
Explain namespaces and PSR-4 folder mapping.

- App\Models\Post and App\Services\PostRepository
- use statements, aliases, leading backslash for global classes
- Why class names must match file paths in PSR-4
- Common autoload error: class not found debugging steps

Tiny tree diagram in comments.

prompts.label: XSS-safe HTML output
prompts.text:
Show escaping user content before echo in HTML.

- htmlspecialchars($s, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') everywhere user data appears
- Example comment form displaying submitted name safely
- Contrast: when you need a HTML purifier library instead
- Content-Security-Policy mention as defense in depth

No framework; plain PHP templates.

prompts.label: password_hash and password_verify
prompts.text:
Teach secure password storage in PHP.

- password_hash on register, password_verify on login
- Never store plaintext or reversible encryption for passwords
- password_needs_rehash when algorithm upgrades
- Minimal register/login flow with PDO (SQLite ok)

Warn against md5/sha1 for passwords.

prompts.label: filter_input and validation
prompts.text:
Validate HTTP input without a framework.

- filter_input(INPUT_POST, 'email', FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL)
- Cast age with FILTER_VALIDATE_INT and options min_max
- Central validateRequest() returning errors array for a form
- Difference between validation and escaping at output

Show error display pattern in HTML.

prompts.label: cURL GET JSON API
prompts.text:
Fetch a public JSON API with cURL in PHP.

- curl_init, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, timeout, User-Agent
- json_decode with JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR
- Handle HTTP status !== 200
- Compare with file_get_contents limitations for HTTPS

Example: jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1.

prompts.label: PHPUnit first test
prompts.text:
Add PHPUnit to a small PHP project.

- composer require --dev phpunit/phpunit
- tests/CalculatorTest.php for add() and divide by zero exception
- phpunit.xml minimal config
- Run vendor/bin/phpunit

Explain arrange-act-assert in one test.

prompts.label: Simple front controller router
prompts.text:
Build a tiny router without Laravel/Symfony.

- public/index.php parses REQUEST_URI and METHOD
- Map GET / and GET /about to callables or controller methods
- 404 plain response for unknown routes
- Explain why docroot should be public/ only

Under 60 lines; no .htaccess required if using php -S with router script note.

prompts.label: Read and write JSON files
prompts.text:
Persist data in a JSON file for learning (not production scale).

- loadUsers() and saveUsers() with file locking flock
- json_encode JSON_PRETTY_PRINT | JSON_UNESCAPED_UNICODE
- Handle corrupt JSON gracefully
- When to move to SQLite instead

CLI or minimal API returning application/json header.

prompts.label: Enums and match expression
prompts.text:
Use backed enum and match in plain PHP 8.1+.

- enum Status: string { case Active = 'active'; ... }
- Function label(Status $s): string with match
- Compare to class constants pattern
- Serialization to database as string value

Short example without framework.

prompts.label: Shared hosting deploy checklist
prompts.text:
Deployment checklist for plain PHP on shared hosting.

- Point domain to public/ only; keep src and .env above web root
- php.ini: display_errors off in production, log errors
- composer install --no-dev on server
- File permissions on uploads and cache dirs
- Never commit .env; use .env.example

Bullet list; mention when to choose VPS + Docker instead.
